flashcards.back: A programming paradigm that organizes code around objects combining data (attributes) and behavior (methods) into single units, modeling real-world entities.

flashcards.front: What is a Class in OOP?
flashcards.back: A blueprint or template that defines the structure (attributes) and behavior (methods) for creating objects. It represents a category of similar objects.

flashcards.front: What is an Object in OOP?
flashcards.back: An instance of a class - a concrete entity created from a class blueprint that occupies memory and can perform actions defined by its class.
The bundling of data (attributes) and methods that operate on that data within a single unit (class), restricting direct access to some components for data protection.
A mechanism where a new class (subclass/derived) inherits properties and behaviors from an existing class (superclass/base), promoting code reusability.
The ability of objects of different classes to be treated as objects of a common superclass. It allows one interface to be used for different data types.
The process of hiding complex implementation details and showing only essential features of an object, focusing on what an object does rather than how.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between Class and Object?
flashcards.back: Class is a blueprint/template defining attributes and methods; Object is a concrete instance of a class that occupies memory and can be manipulated.

flashcards.front: What is a Constructor in Java?
flashcards.back: A special method used to initialize new objects. It has the same name as the class and is called automatically when an object is created.

flashcards.front: What is a Method in OOP?
flashcards.back: A function defined inside a class that describes the behavior/actions an object can perform. It operates on object data.
A variable that stores the memory address (reference) of an object. In Java, objects are accessed through reference variables.

flashcards.front: What is the 'this' keyword in Java?
flashcards.back: A reference variable that refers to the current object instance within a method or constructor, used to distinguish instance variables from local parameters.

flashcards.front: What is a package in Java?
flashcards.back: A namespace that organizes related classes and interfaces, providing access protection and namespace management.

flashcards.front: What are the advantages of OOP?
flashcards.back: Modularity, reusability, maintainability, extensibility, data hiding, easier debugging, and better mapping to real-world entities.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between OOP and Procedural Programming?
flashcards.back: OOP organizes code around objects (data+methods); Procedural programming organizes code around functions and sequential data. OOP models real-world entities better.
